picks.title: Nanocoder as an ACP agent: driving it from Zed
picks.why: The v1.28.0 ACP-for-Zed write-up has the deepest technical meat of any candidate, explaining why this is effectively a new agent (same Nanocoder, editor-native UI) and contrasting it with the VS Code extension, which is the kind of architectural walk-through GitHub Discussion readers value.

A positioning post travels well on Reddit because it invites debate rather than just announcing a feature, and the before/after framing of the Nanocoder tagline is a self-contained discussion prompt that does not depend on readers running the tool.

ACP for Zed is the kind of milestone LinkedIn audiences forward, with concrete UX details (streaming, tool cards, diffs, permissions) that ship well as a professional-facing announcement from the most recent release.

The auto-tune profile hook (33 to 19 tools, size-suffix inference, mid-session re-resolution) is dense, concrete, and tweet-shaped, which is exactly the kind of single-concrete-number post that performs on X.
